headings: h3: Martin Vickers Conservative candidate for Brigg and Immingham
text_excerpt: About Martin | Martin Vickers Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Martin News My Speeches Advice Surgeries Campaigns Contact About Martin Tweet Greetings! Thank you for taking the time to visit my website and learn a little more about me. I was born and raised in Cleethorpes, being educated locally at Havelock School and Grimsby College. In 2004, after 6 years of study as a part-time student at Lincoln University, I earned my B.A. degree in politics with honours. I have always lived in the Grimsby/Cleethorpes area, and have been a passionate supporter of Grimsby Town since my first game in the Osmond stand versus Norwich in 1959 with my parents. Prior to taking up the position of Constituency Agent to Edward Leigh MP in 1994, I worked in the printing industry and retail trade in the Cleethorpes area. My passion for public service has given me 25 years of experience as a councillor, representing Scartho Ward in North East Lincolnshire, including six years as the Cabinet Member for Environmental Services. I was first elected as a Member of Parliament in May 2010 and was delighted to be re-elected to parliament as the MP for Brigg and Immingham in 2024. Along with serving as a local councillor I have previously served the local community as a churchwarden. Outside of politics I enjoy spending time with my wife and daughter, reading (mainly politics, history and religion), railways, travel, the Church/religion, football & cricket, and music. Please feel free to let me know when I can be of service.
